title: Huge INSERT Statements Breaking Imports? Split Them Safely
description: Large INSERT blocks cause packet errors and timeouts. Here’s how to split a dump without damage.
author: SQLSplit

content.text: A single INSERT can contain thousands of rows. If it’s too big, imports fail with packet errors.
max_allowed_packet and timeouts are triggered by oversized statements.

items: Split by size or line count
Then import data chunks in order

content.text: Related: **MySQL max_allowed_packet Error: What It Means and How to Fix It**.

content.title: Split big INSERTs
content.text: Keep your import stable by reducing statement size.

faq.question: Can I split inside an INSERT safely?
faq.answer: Only if the splitter is SQL‑aware. Otherwise you’ll corrupt the statement.
